+{"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-hiff","title":"CLI: Add sync command to access premium library","description":"## Task
Add a sync command to the jfp CLI to download/sync prompts from the user's premium library.

## Context
Authenticated premium users should be able to sync their personal prompt library to local machine for offline access and faster searching.

## Commands
- jfp sync - Sync all saved prompts to local cache
- jfp sync --force - Force full re-sync
- jfp sync --status - Show sync status

## Local Cache
- Location: ~/.jeffreysprompts/library/
- Format: JSON files matching premium prompt schema
- Metadata: Last sync timestamp, prompt IDs

## Integration Points
- Premium API: /api/cli/sync (from premium project)
- Requires authentication (see jfp login)

## Offline Support
- Synced prompts available offline
- Search works on local cache
- Flag shows which prompts are from premium vs free tier

## Success Criteria
- Full library synced to local machine
- Incremental sync for efficiency
- Works offline after initial sync
- Clear feedback during sync","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-11T00:30:41.167692981-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-11T00:30:41.167692981-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-hiff","depends_on_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-uw7z","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-11T00:31:35.90917947-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-hmi","title":"Build transcript helper components (InsightCard, MessageContent, TimelineSkeleton)","description":"**Details:**
Three supporting components for the How It Was Made page:

**1. InsightCard (insight-card.tsx)**
- Displays TranscriptHighlight with type-specific styling
- Icons: Brain (key_decision), Lightbulb (interesting_prompt), CheckCircle (clever_solution), AlertTriangle (lesson_learned)
- Color-coded background and border per type
- Shows annotation text

**2. MessageContent (message-content.tsx)**
- Renders markdown-like content with code blocks
- Uses react-syntax-highlighter with oneDark theme
- Splits content on \`\`\` delimiters
- Detects language from fence or content heuristics

**3. TimelineSkeleton (timeline-skeleton.tsx)**
- Loading skeleton for timeline
- Animated pulse effect
- Matches timeline structure (circles + message placeholders)

**Location:** apps/web/src/components/transcript/

**Reasoning:** Supporting components keep TranscriptTimeline and MessageDetail clean. Enables Suspense boundaries with meaningful loading states.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-10T00:33:24.934599998-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-10T16:49:48.421638564-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-10T16:49:48.421638564-05:00","close_reason":"All helper components implemented: InsightCard, MessageContent, TimelineSkeleton, StatsDashboardSkeleton","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-hmi","depends_on_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-vsk","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-10T00:38:11.094367041-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

+{"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-rd99","title":"Document CLI-Premium integration architecture","description":"## Task
Document the integration architecture between the free CLI (jfp) and the premium SaaS backend.

## Documentation Sections

### 1. Authentication Flow
- Device code flow (RFC 8628) explanation
- Token storage and refresh
- Error handling

### 2. API Endpoints
- /api/cli/device-code - Initiate auth
- /api/cli/token - Exchange code for token
- /api/cli/sync - Sync library
- /api/cli/collections - Manage collections

### 3. Local Cache Structure
- ~/.jeffreysprompts/ directory layout
- credentials.json format
- library/ cache structure
- Sync metadata

### 4. Prompt Format Compatibility
- Free tier prompt format (TypeScript)
- Premium prompt format (database)
- Conversion between formats
- SKILL.md export format

### 5. Offline Mode
- What works offline
- Cache invalidation
- Conflict resolution

## Files to Create
- docs/cli-premium-integration.md
- docs/api-reference.md
- Update README.md with premium features section

## Success Criteria
- Clear documentation for contributors
- API contracts documented
- Cache format specified
- Troubleshooting guide included","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-11T00:31:23.09441384-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-11T00:31:23.09441384-05:00"}

 {"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-rnm","title":"Implement useLocalStorage hook","description":"**Details:**
- Generic hook for localStorage persistence
- SSR-safe (handles hydration)
- Type-safe with generics
- Used by: basket, theme, variable inputs, preferences

**Implementation:**
```typescript
// apps/web/src/hooks/use-local-storage.ts
'use client';

export function useLocalStorage<T>(key: string, initialValue: T) {
  // State to store our value
  const [storedValue, setStoredValue] = useState<T>(() => {
    if (typeof window === 'undefined') return initialValue;
    try {
      const item = localStorage.getItem(key);
      return item ? JSON.parse(item) : initialValue;
    } catch {
      return initialValue;
    }
  });

  // Persist to localStorage on change
  useEffect(() => {
    if (typeof window === 'undefined') return;
    try {
      localStorage.setItem(key, JSON.stringify(storedValue));
    } catch (error) {
      console.warn('Failed to save to localStorage:', error);
    }
  }, [key, storedValue]);

  return [storedValue, setStoredValue] as const;
}
```

**SSR Handling:**
- Initial render uses provided default
- useEffect syncs with localStorage after hydration
- Prevents hydration mismatch errors

**Location:** apps/web/src/hooks/use-local-storage.ts

**Reasoning:** Many features need persistent state (basket, theme, variable inputs). Centralizing localStorage logic in a hook ensures consistent behavior and SSR safety.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-10T00:34:35.714509321-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-10T01:44:46.164609393-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-10T01:44:46.164609393-05:00","close_reason":"Added apps/web/src/hooks/use-local-storage.ts SSR-safe localStorage hook"}

+{"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-uw7z","title":"CLI: Add premium login command for authenticated access","description":"## Task
Add a login command to the jfp CLI to authenticate with JeffreysPrompts Premium.

## Context
The jfp CLI currently accesses only the static free-tier prompts. Premium users should be able to:
- Authenticate their CLI to access their personal library
- Sync saved prompts to local machine
- Access premium-only content

## Implementation
Use the device code flow (RFC 8628) that the premium project implements:
1. jfp login → opens browser to premium verification page
2. User confirms in browser
3. CLI receives token and stores securely
4. Subsequent commands include auth token

## Integration Points
- Premium API: /api/cli/device-code (from premium project)
- Premium API: /api/cli/token (from premium project)
- Local storage: ~/.jeffreysprompts/credentials.json

## Commands
- jfp login - Start device code flow
- jfp logout - Clear stored credentials
- jfp whoami - Show current authenticated user
- jfp status - Show auth status and subscription tier

## Success Criteria
- CLI can authenticate to premium API
- Credentials stored securely
- Token refresh handled automatically
- Clear error messages for auth failures","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-11T00:30:26.577649305-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-11T00:30:26.577649305-05:00"}

 {"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-w48","title":"Build Workflow Builder UI component","description":"Part 7.7 of PLAN. Key differentiator feature. Let users assemble multi-step workflows: choose prompts as steps from search, drag-to-reorder steps, add short 'handoff notes' between steps, export as markdown or skill bundle. Persists work-in-progress to localStorage. +{"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-wlg3","title":"CLI: Add collections command for premium users","description":"## Task
Add a collections command to the jfp CLI for premium users to browse and use their collections.

## Commands
- jfp collections - List user's collections
- jfp collections <name> - Show prompts in collection
- jfp collections <name> --export - Export collection to files
- jfp collections create <name> - Create new collection (syncs to premium)
- jfp collections add <collection> <prompt-id> - Add prompt to collection

## Integration Points
- Premium API: /api/cli/collections (from premium project)
- Requires authentication

## Output Formats
- Default: Pretty table with prompt counts
- --json: JSON output for scripting
- --export: SKILL.md files or markdown

## Success Criteria
- List and browse collections
- Export collections locally
- Create and modify collections (syncs to cloud)
- Works with synced local cache","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-11T00:30:54.686074336-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-11T00:30:54.686074336-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-wlg3","depends_on_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-uw7z","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-11T00:31:41.066778838-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-wlg3","depends_on_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-hiff","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-11T00:31:46.195827853-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
